steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The number of other invertebrates that may inhabit the CCZ is currently ______ because sampling is poor and the few samples taken cannot be assumed to be representative. The blank must describe a quantity that cannot be reliably determined — something like "impossible to estimate" or "unknowable."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — infinitesimal: Means extremely small. The passage says we cannot determine the number, not that it is small. ✗
  • Choice B — verifiable: Means capable of being confirmed. The passage says the opposite — the data is unreliable and unrepresentative. ✗
  • Choice C — tenuous: Means weak or flimsy. A number cannot be tenuous; this describes a relationship or argument, not a quantity. ✗
  • Choice D — inestimable: Means impossible to estimate or calculate. Substituting it in: "the number... is currently inestimable" — directly matches the reason given (poor, unrepresentative sampling). ✓
That confirms D is correct.
